name: Woodburning Ovens
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour a cap on the foundation wall to allow bricks to be laid flush with the hearth.
2. Mix concrete from scratch using white cement, sand, and pea gravel.
3. Pour three slabs for the front of the oven, each 2 inches thick and protruding 1 inch beyond the previous slab.
4. Stand all wall bricks up, spacing them 1/4 inch apart with a china pencil.
5. Mix mortar and apply it between bricks, creating a 1/4 mortar bed on skinny sides facing in.
6. Lay the throat using two lines of four courses, each just 1&1/2 common bricks long protruding from jamb bricks.
7. Build a form for the arch using plywood cut from a pattern made by placing bricks on wood in the desired shape and tracing their outline.
8. Mix mortar and apply it to support the arch, ensuring it is level behind the angle iron.
9. Consider using technical plans and information from sources such as Oven Crafters, TMR Linea Bar, Maurice Sabbagh's Earthstone Ovens, or "The Bread Ovens of Quebec" by Lise Boily and Jean-Francois Blanchette.
Note: This recipe requires knowledge of masonry skills to build the oven correctly. It is recommended to follow professional plans for specific construction details.
